guru_daa-complete_868.title: 10.1 Golden Rule
guru_daa-complete_868.content:
**"1 MESSAGE = ALL RELATED OPERATIONS"**

All operations must be concurrent/parallel in a single message:
- TodoWrite: Batch ALL todos in ONE call
- Task tool: Spawn ALL agents in ONE message
- File operations: Batch ALL reads/writes/edits
- Bash commands: Batch ALL terminal operations
- Memory operations: Batch ALL memory store/retrieve

guru_daa-complete_879.title: 10.2 Agent Coordination Protocol
guru_daa-complete_879.content:
**Before Work:**
```bash
npx claude-flow@alpha hooks pre-task --description "[task]"
npx claude-flow@alpha hooks session-restore --session-id "swarm-[id]"
```

**During Work:**
```bash
npx claude-flow@alpha hooks post-edit --file "[file]" --memory-key "swarm/[agent]/[step]"
npx claude-flow@alpha hooks notify --message "[what was done]"
```

**After Work:**
```bash
npx claude-flow@alpha hooks post-task --task-id "[task]"
npx claude-flow@alpha hooks session-end --export-metrics true
```

guru_dspy-ts-complete_2.title: Overview
guru_dspy-ts-complete_2.content:
**DSPy.ts** is a TypeScript implementation of Stanford's DSPy framework - a paradigm shift from "prompting" to "programming" AI systems. Instead of manually crafting prompts, DSPy.ts lets you define modular, composable programs that can be automatically optimized.

**Core Philosophy**: "Program AI Systems, Don't Prompt Them"

**Repository**: `/tmp/ruvnet-repos/dspy.ts/`
**Version**: 2.0.0
**License**: MIT

---

guru_dspy-ts-complete_62.title: Key Components
guru_dspy-ts-complete_62.content:
| Component | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| **Signatures** | Type-safe specifications for module inputs/outputs |
| **Modules** | Composable units (Predict, ChainOfThought, ReAct) |
| **Optimizers** | Automatic prompt/weight optimization |
| **Pipelines** | Chain modules with retry logic |
| **AgentDB** | Vector database (150x faster than baseline) |
| **ReasoningBank** | Self-learning memory with SAFLA algorithm |
| **Swarm** | Multi-agent orchestration |

---

guru_dspy-ts-complete_78.title: The DSPy Paradigm
guru_dspy-ts-complete_78.content:
Traditional approach:
```
User -> Prompt Engineering -> LLM -> Output
```

DSPy approach:
```
User -> Define Signature -> Create Module -> Optimize -> Deploy
```

guru_dspy-ts-complete_90.title: Benefits
guru_dspy-ts-complete_90.content:
1. **Declarative**: Define what you want, not how to get it
2. **Composable**: Chain modules together
3. **Optimizable**: Automatic improvement through training
4. **Type-Safe**: Full TypeScript support
5. **Testable**: Unit test your AI programs

guru_dspy-ts-complete_1296.title: Summary
guru_dspy-ts-complete_1296.content:
DSPy.ts 2.0 provides a complete TypeScript framework for building declarative, self-improving AI programs:

1. **Signatures** define type-safe I/O contracts
2. **Modules** (Predict, ChainOfThought, ReAct) implement reasoning patterns
3. **Optimizers** (BootstrapFewShot) automatically improve performance
4. **Pipelines** chain modules with retry logic
5. **AgentDB** provides 150x faster vector search
6. **ReasoningBank** enables self-learning with SAFLA
7. **Swarm** orchestrates multi-agent coordination

The framework is production-ready for core use cases and actively being extended with advanced optimizers and modules.

---

**Documentation Extracted**: 2025-12-29
**DSPy.ts Version**: 2.0.0

Built entirely on the **MCP (Model Context Protocol)** standard, it enables recursive intelligence where agents spawn agents, sandboxes create sandboxes, and systems improve themselves.

guru_ruvector-complete_8.title: Overview
guru_ruvector-complete_8.content: **RuVector is a distributed vector database that learns.** It combines vector storage, graph queries with Cypher, horizontal scaling with Raft consensus, and self-improving search through Graph Neural Networks (GNN).

guru_ruvector-complete_12.title: Core Value Proposition
guru_ruvector-complete_12.content:
Think of RuVector as: **Pinecone + Neo4j + PyTorch + postgres + etcd** in one Rust package.

```bash
npx ruvector
```

guru_ruvector-complete_20.title: What Makes RuVector Different
guru_ruvector-complete_20.content:
Traditional vector databases just store and search. RuVector:

1. **Stores vectors** like any vector DB (embeddings from OpenAI, Cohere, etc.)
2. **Queries with Cypher** like Neo4j (`MATCH (a)-[:SIMILAR]->(b) RETURN b`)
3. **The index learns** - GNN layers make search results improve over time
4. **Scales horizontally** - Raft consensus, multi-master replication, auto-sharding
5. **Routes AI requests** - Semantic routing and FastGRNN neural inference for LLM optimization
6. **Compresses automatically** - 2-32x memory reduction with adaptive tiered compression
7. **39 attention mechanisms** - Flash, linear, graph, hyperbolic for custom models
8. **Drops into Postgres** - pgvector-compatible extension with SIMD acceleration
9. **Runs anywhere** - Node.js, browser (WASM), HTTP server, or native Rust
10. **Continuous learning** - SONA enables runtime adaptation with LoRA, EWC++, and ReasoningBank

guru_ruvector-complete_105.title: Vector Search
guru_ruvector-complete_105.content:
| Feature | What It Does | Why It Matters |
|---------|--------------|----------------|
| **HNSW Indexing** | O(log n) approximate nearest neighbor search | Fast enough for real-time apps |
| **Multiple Distance Metrics** | Euclidean, Cosine, Dot Product, Manhattan | Choose best for your embeddings |
| **Advanced Quantization** | Scalar (4x), Product (8-32x), Binary (32x) | Memory-efficient storage |
| **SIMD Optimizations** | Hardware-accelerated via SimSIMD | 200-300x speedup |
| **Zero-Copy I/O** | Memory-mapped storage | Instant loading |
| **Metadata Filtering** | Filter vectors by properties | Combine semantic + structured search |
| **Collections** | Namespace isolation, multi-tenancy | Organize by project/user |

guru_ruvector-complete_117.title: Graph Neural Networks
guru_ruvector-complete_117.content:
Traditional vector search:
```
Query -> HNSW Index -> Top K Results
```

RuVector with GNN:
```
Query -> HNSW Index -> GNN Layer -> Enhanced Results
              ^                          |
              +------ learns from -------+
```

The GNN layer:
1. Takes your query and its nearest neighbors
2. Applies multi-head attention to weigh which neighbors matter
3. Updates representations based on graph structure
4. Returns better-ranked results

Over time, frequently-accessed paths get reinforced, making common queries faster and more accurate.

guru_ruvector-complete_139.title: Cypher Query Language
guru_ruvector-complete_139.content:
RuVector implements Neo4j-compatible Cypher with hyperedge extensions:

```cypher
-- Create nodes
CREATE (p:Person {name: 'Alice', age: 30})

-- Create relationships
CREATE (a:Person {name: 'Alice'})-[:KNOWS]->(b:Person {name: 'Bob'})

-- Pattern matching
MATCH (p:Person)-[:KNOWS]->(friend)
WHERE p.name = 'Alice'
RETURN friend.name

-- Hyperedges (N-ary relationships)
CREATE (a:Person)-[:ATTENDED]->(meeting:Meeting, room:Room, company:Company)
```

**Supported Clauses:** MATCH, CREATE, MERGE, SET, DELETE, RETURN, WITH, WHERE, ORDER BY, SKIP, LIMIT

**Operators:** Arithmetic (+, -, *, /, %, ^), Comparison (=, <>, <, >, <=, >=), Logical (AND, OR, NOT, XOR), String (STARTS WITH, ENDS WITH, CONTAINS)

**Aggregations:** COUNT, AVG, MIN, MAX, SUM, COLLECT

guru_ruvector-complete_165.title: Distributed Systems (Raft Consensus)
guru_ruvector-complete_165.content:
| Feature | What It Does | Why It Matters |
|---------|--------------|----------------|
| **Raft Consensus** | Leader election, log replication | Strong consistency for metadata |
| **Auto-Sharding** | Consistent hashing, shard migration | Scale to billions of vectors |
| **Multi-Master Replication** | Write to any node, conflict resolution | High availability, no SPOF |
| **Snapshots** | Point-in-time backups, incremental | Disaster recovery |
| **Cluster Metrics** | Prometheus-compatible monitoring | Observability at scale |

**Raft Configuration Example:**
```rust
let config = RaftNodeConfig {
    node_id: "node-1".into(),
    cluster_members: vec!["node-1", "node-2", "node-3", "node-4", "node-5"]
        .into_iter().map(Into::into).collect(),
    election_timeout_min: 150,  // ms
    election_timeout_max: 300,  // ms
    heartbeat_interval: 50,     // ms
};
let raft = RaftNode::new(config);
```

guru_ruvector-complete_215.title: Compression Tiers
guru_ruvector-complete_215.content:
The architecture adapts to your data automatically. Hot paths get full precision; cold paths compress automatically.

| Access Frequency | Format | Compression | What Happens |
|-----------------|--------|-------------|--------------|
| **Hot** (>80%) | f32 | 1x | Full precision, instant retrieval |
| **Warm** (40-80%) | f16 | 2x | Slight compression, imperceptible latency |
| **Cool** (10-40%) | PQ8 | 8x | Smart quantization, ~1ms overhead |
| **Cold** (1-10%) | PQ4 | 16x | Heavy compression, still fast search |
| **Archive** (<1%) | Binary | 32x | Maximum density, batch retrieval |

**Memory Usage (1M Vectors, 384 Dimensions):**

| Configuration | Memory | Recall |
|---------------|--------|--------|
| Full Precision (f32) | ~1.5GB | 100% |
| Scalar Quantization | ~400MB | 98% |
| Product Quantization | ~200MB | 95% |
| Binary Quantization | ~50MB | 85% |

guru_ruvector-complete_240.title: Core Attention
guru_ruvector-complete_240.content:
| Mechanism | Complexity | Memory | Best For |
|-----------|------------|--------|----------|
| **DotProductAttention** | O(n^2) | O(n^2) | Basic attention for small-medium sequences |
| **MultiHeadAttention** | O(n^2*h) | O(n^2*h) | BERT, GPT-style transformers |
| **FlashAttention** | O(n^2) | O(n) | Long sequences with limited GPU memory |
| **LinearAttention** | O(n*d) | O(n*d) | 8K+ token sequences, real-time streaming |
| **HyperbolicAttention** | O(n^2) | O(n^2) | Tree-like data: taxonomies, org charts |
| **MoEAttention** | O(n*k) | O(n*k) | Large models with sparse expert routing |

guru_ruvector-complete_251.title: Graph Attention
guru_ruvector-complete_251.content:
| Mechanism | Complexity | Best For |
|-----------|------------|----------|
| **GraphRoPeAttention** | O(n^2) | Position-aware graph transformers |
| **EdgeFeaturedAttention** | O(n^2*e) | Molecules, knowledge graphs with edge data |
| **DualSpaceAttention** | O(n^2) | Hybrid flat + hierarchical embeddings |
| **LocalGlobalAttention** | O(n*k + n) | 100K+ node graphs, scalable GNNs |

guru_ruvector-complete_260.title: Specialized
guru_ruvector-complete_260.content:
| Mechanism | Type | Best For |
|-----------|------|----------|
| **SparseAttention** | Efficiency | Long docs, low-memory inference |
| **CrossAttention** | Multi-modal | Image-text, encoder-decoder models |
| **NeighborhoodAttention** | Graph | Local message passing in GNNs |
| **HierarchicalAttention** | Structure | Multi-level docs (section -> paragraph) |

guru_ruvector-complete_269.title: Hyperbolic Math Functions
guru_ruvector-complete_269.content:
For Poincare ball embeddings - curved space that naturally represents hierarchies:

| Function | Description | Use Case |
|----------|-------------|----------|
| `expMap(v, c)` | Map to hyperbolic space | Initialize embeddings |
| `logMap(p, c)` | Map to flat space | Compute gradients |
| `mobiusAddition(x, y, c)` | Add vectors in curved space | Aggregate features |
| `poincareDistance(x, y, c)` | Measure hyperbolic distance | Compute similarity |
| `projectToPoincareBall(p, c)` | Ensure valid coordinates | Prevent numerical errors |

guru_ruvector-complete_287.title: Features

- **ReasoningBank**: K-means++ clustering stores and retrieves successful reasoning patterns

guru_ruvllm-complete_34.title: Overview
guru_ruvllm-complete_34.content:
RuvLLM is a **self-learning LLM orchestration system** that combines:

- **SONA** (Self-Optimizing Neural Architecture) for adaptive learning
- **HNSW** (Hierarchical Navigable Small World) graphs for efficient memory
- **FastGRNN** for intelligent model routing
- **SIMD** acceleration for high-performance inference
- **LoRA/EWC** for continual learning without catastrophic forgetting
- **Federated Learning** for distributed, privacy-preserving training

guru_ruvllm-complete_45.title: Key Features
guru_ruvllm-complete_45.content:
| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Self-Learning** | Automatically adapts from interactions via SONA |
| **Memory-Augmented** | HNSW graph for O(log N) similarity search |
| **Multi-Model Routing** | FastGRNN routes to optimal models |
| **High Performance** | SIMD vectorization (AVX2/AVX512/NEON) |
| **Continual Learning** | LoRA adapters + EWC++ prevent forgetting |
| **Federated** | Distributed learning with privacy guarantees |
| **Edge-Ready** | WASM support for browser/edge deployment |

guru_ruvllm-complete_108.content: **SONA (Self-Optimizing Neural Architecture)** is RuvLLM's core adaptive learning system. It implements three temporal learning loops that work together to continuously improve performance.

guru_safla-complete_28.title: Overview
guru_safla-complete_28.content:
SAFLA (Self-Aware Feedback Loop Algorithm) is a custom-built neural network designed specifically for AI agents and autonomous coding systems. It enhances AI agents and automated coding systems like Claude Code with:

- **Persistent Memory**: Never loses context across sessions
- **Self-Learning Capabilities**: Performance improves with each interaction
- **Adaptive Reasoning**: Learns what works best for different tasks
- **Built-in Safety**: Prevents harmful or dangerous operations
- **High Performance**: 172k+ operations/sec

guru_safla-complete_38.title: Key Benefits
guru_safla-complete_38.content:
| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Persistent Memory** | Remembers everything between sessions, smart recall, learns patterns |
| **Self-Learning** | Gets smarter over time, adapts strategies, continuous optimization |
| **Safe & Reliable** | Built-in safety constraints, rollback capability, real-time monitoring |
| **High Performance** | 172k+ ops/sec, real-time response, efficient memory compression |

guru_safla-complete_47.title: Use Cases
guru_safla-complete_47.content:
- **Claude Code** - MCP integration for autonomous development
- **Research Agents** - Persistent memory and knowledge building
- **Autonomous Systems** - Self-improving AI workflows
- **Enterprise AI** - Production-ready agent orchestration

guru_safla-complete_100.title: System Architecture
guru_safla-complete_100.content: SAFLA implements a layered architecture with clear separation of concerns, enabling scalable, maintainable, and safe autonomous AI operations.

guru_safla-complete_104.title: Architectural Layers
guru_safla-complete_104.content:
```
Application Layer
  ├── REST API
  ├── Command Line Interface
  ├── Python SDK
  └── Web Interface

Orchestration Layer
  ├── MCP Orchestrator
  ├── Agent Coordinator
  ├── Task Manager
  └── Workflow Engine

Cognitive Layer
  ├── Meta-Cognitive Engine
  ├── Goal Manager
  ├── Strategy Selector
  ├── Performance Monitor
  └── Adaptation Engine

Memory Layer
  ├── Vector Memory
  ├── Episodic Memory
  ├── Semantic Memory
  ├── Working Memory
  └── Memory Consolidation

Safety Layer
  ├── Safety Constraints
  ├── Validation Pipeline
  ├── Risk Assessment
  ├── Rollback Mechanisms
  └── Safety Monitoring

Infrastructure Layer
  ├── Storage Systems
  ├── Compute Resources
  ├── Network Services
  └── Security Services
```

guru_safla-complete_147.title: Design Principles
guru_safla-complete_147.content:
1. **Safety First** - All operations validated before execution, multiple safety layers
2. **Modularity** - Clear separation of concerns, well-defined interfaces
3. **Scalability** - Horizontal and vertical scaling capabilities
4. **Reliability** - Fault tolerance, error recovery, redundancy
5. **Maintainability** - Clean code, comprehensive logging, configuration-driven
6. **Security** - Multi-layer architecture, encryption, access control

guru_safla-complete_156.title: Architectural Patterns
guru_safla-complete_156.content:
- **Layered Architecture**: Separation of concerns with abstraction layers
- **Event-Driven Architecture**: Asynchronous processing, loose coupling
- **Microservices Pattern**: Service independence, technology diversity
- **Circuit Breaker Pattern**: Fault tolerance, quick recovery

guru_safla-complete_165.title: Hybrid Memory System
guru_safla-complete_165.content: SAFLA's hybrid memory architecture is inspired by human cognitive memory systems, implementing four specialized memory types that work together.

guru_safla-complete_169.title: Memory Types
guru_safla-complete_169.content:
#### 1. Vector Memory System

High-dimensional representations enabling similarity-based retrieval and semantic understanding.

**Features:**
- Multi-dimensional embeddings: 512, 768, 1024, 1536 dimensions
- Multiple similarity metrics: cosine, euclidean, dot product, manhattan
- Efficient indexing: HNSW, IVF, LSH, Flat Index
- Compression: Product Quantization, Scalar Quantization, Binary Quantization

**Performance Targets:**
- Vector search: <1ms for 10k vectors
- Batch operations: <10ms for 1000 vectors
- Memory efficiency: >80% storage optimization

```python
from safla.core.hybrid_memory import HybridMemoryArchitecture

memory = HybridMemoryArchitecture(
    vector_config={"embedding_dim": 768, "max_capacity": 10000},
    episodic_config={"max_capacity": 1000},
    working_config={"capacity": 10, "attention_window": 5}
)

guru_safla-complete_195.title: Store and search
guru_safla-complete_195.content:
memory_id = await memory.store_vector_memory(
    content="Machine learning is a subset of AI",
    embedding=embedding_vector,
    metadata={"type": "definition", "domain": "AI"}
)

similar_memories = await memory.search_similar_memories(
    query_embedding=query_vector,
    similarity_metric=SimilarityMetric.COSINE,
    top_k=10,
    similarity_threshold=0.8
)
```

#### 2. Episodic Memory System

Sequential experiences with temporal context for learning from past interactions.

**Features:**
- Sequential experience storage with temporal ordering
- Context-outcome relationship tracking
- Event clustering and pattern recognition
- Causal relationship inference
- Experience replay for learning enhancement

```python
episode_id = await memory.store_episodic_memory(
    content="User requested explanation of neural networks",
    context={
        "user_id": "user_123",
        "session_id": "session_456",
        "previous_topics": ["machine_learning", "algorithms"]
    },
    outcome="provided_comprehensive_explanation",
    metadata={"duration": 180, "satisfaction_score": 0.9}
)

guru_safla-complete_233.title: Temporal queries
guru_safla-complete_233.content:
recent_episodes = await memory.get_episodes_by_timerange(
    start_time=datetime.now() - timedelta(hours=24),
    end_time=datetime.now()
)
```

#### 3. Semantic Memory System

Knowledge graph for storing structured information and concept relationships.

**Features:**
- Graph-based knowledge representation
- Weighted relationship modeling
- Multi-hop inference capabilities
- Dynamic knowledge expansion
- Concept hierarchy management

**Graph Algorithms:**
- Traversal: BFS, DFS, Dijkstra's, PageRank
- Inference: Rule-based, Probabilistic, Analogical, Causal

```python

guru_safla-complete_256.title: Add concept nodes

guru_safla-complete_263.title: Create relationships
guru_safla-complete_263.content:
await memory.add_semantic_edge(
    source_id=ml_node,
    target_id=ai_node,
    relationship="is_subset_of",
    weight=0.9
)

guru_safla-complete_271.title: Query knowledge graph
guru_safla-complete_271.content:
related_concepts = await memory.get_related_nodes(
    node_id=ai_node,
    relationship_types=["is_subset_of", "related_to"],
    max_depth=3
)
```

#### 4. Working Memory System

Active context and immediate processing with attention mechanisms.

**Features:**
- Limited capacity (7 plus/minus 2 items, configurable)
- Attention-based focus management
- Temporal decay for automatic cleanup
- Integration with long-term memory
- Real-time context maintenance

```python
attention_config = {
    "capacity_limit": 7,
    "decay_rate": 0.1,
    "rehearsal_strength": 0.8,
    "priority_weights": {"relevance": 0.4, "recency": 0.3, "importance": 0.3}
}

await memory.working_memory.add_item(
    content="Current user query",
    priority=0.9,
    attention_weight=0.8
)

active_context = await memory.working_memory.get_active_context(max_items=5)
```

guru_safla-complete_307.title: Memory Consolidation
guru_safla-complete_307.content:
The consolidation engine manages transfer between memory systems based on importance, frequency, and relevance.

**Phases:**
1. Assessment: Importance scoring, access frequency, relevance analysis, novelty detection
2. Decision: Threshold evaluation, memory type routing, conflict resolution
3. Transfer: Information extraction, format transformation, integration, verification
4. Optimization: Data compression, index updates, cleanup operations

```python
await memory.consolidation_engine.consolidate_memories(
    source_memory="working",
    target_memories=["vector", "episodic", "semantic"],
    criteria={
        "min_importance": 0.8,
        "min_access_count": 3,
        "max_age": timedelta(hours=1)
    }
)
```

guru_safla-complete_331.title: Meta-Cognitive Engine
guru_safla-complete_331.content: The Meta-Cognitive Engine provides self-awareness and strategic reasoning, enabling autonomous learning, goal management, strategy selection, and adaptive behavior.

guru_safla-complete_335.title: Components
guru_safla-complete_335.content:
1. **Self-Awareness Layer**
   - Self-Model: Skills, limitations, resources, performance
   - State Monitoring: Current state, context, environment
   - Capability Assessment: Continuous skill evaluation
   - Performance Tracking: Metrics and trends

2. **Goal Management Layer**
   - Goal Hierarchy: Mission > Vision > Strategic > Operational > Task
   - Priority Management: Critical, High, Medium, Low
   - Goal Decomposition: Automatic breakdown into sub-goals
   - Progress Tracking: Success criteria evaluation

3. **Strategy Layer**
   - Strategy Repository: Learned, Template, Adaptive, Emergency strategies
   - Strategy Selection: Context matching, resource requirements, success probability
   - Plan Generation: Execution planning with resource allocation
   - Adaptation: Real-time strategy refinement

4. **Learning Layer**
   - Experience Capture: Action logging, outcome tracking
   - Pattern Recognition: Success/failure patterns, context patterns
   - Knowledge Extraction: Rule extraction, heuristic generation
   - Skill Development: Continuous capability enhancement

guru_safla-complete_456.title: Safety & Validation Framework
guru_safla-complete_456.content: SAFLA's safety framework implements comprehensive validation, risk assessment, and rollback capabilities for secure autonomous operation.

guru_safla-complete_460.title: Multi-Layer Safety Architecture
guru_safla-complete_460.content:
```
Input Safety Layer
  ├── Input Validation
  ├── Data Sanitization
  ├── Rate Limiting
  └── Authentication

Process Safety Layer
  ├── Safety Constraints
  ├── Real-time Monitoring
  ├── Circuit Breakers
  └── Timeout Controls

Output Safety Layer
  ├── Output Validation
  ├── Content Filtering
  ├── Approval Gates
  └── Audit Logging

System Safety Layer
  ├── Resource Limits
  ├── Emergency Stop
  ├── Rollback System
  └── Recovery Procedures
```

guru_safla-complete_488.title: Safety Constraints Engine
guru_safla-complete_488.content:
**Constraint Types:**
- **Hard Constraints**: Cannot be violated, emergency stop on breach
- **Soft Constraints**: Warnings and adjustments allowed
- **Dynamic Constraints**: Context-aware, adaptive limits

```python
from safla.core.safety_validation import SafetyConstraint, ConstraintType, ViolationAction

guru_safla-complete_498.title: Hard constraint
guru_safla-complete_498.content:
memory_constraint = SafetyConstraint(
    name="memory_limit",
    constraint_type=ConstraintType.HARD,
    rule="memory_usage <= 1000000000",  # 1GB
    threshold=1000000000,
    violation_action=ViolationAction.EMERGENCY_STOP,
    priority=1
)

guru_safla-complete_508.title: Soft constraint
guru_safla-complete_508.content:
performance_constraint = SafetyConstraint(
    name="response_time",
    constraint_type=ConstraintType.SOFT,
    rule="response_time <= 5.0",
    threshold=5.0,
    violation_action=ViolationAction.WARNING,
    priority=3
)

guru_safla-complete_522.title: Validation Pipeline
guru_safla-complete_522.content:
Multi-stage validation with comprehensive error handling:

1. **Pre-Processing Validation**: Input format, schema, range, type
2. **Security Validation**: Authentication, authorization, injection prevention
3. **Business Logic Validation**: Rules, consistency, dependencies, workflow
4. **Post-Processing Validation**: Output validation, quality, compliance

```python
from safla.core.safety_validation import ValidationPipeline, ValidationStage

pipeline = ValidationPipeline(
    timeout=30.0,
    fail_fast=False,
    parallel_execution=True
)

validation_result = await pipeline.execute(data=request_data, context=context)

if validation_result.is_valid:
    proceed_with_operation(validation_result.validated_data)
```

guru_safla-complete_546.title: Risk Assessment Engine
guru_safla-complete_546.content:
Quantifies potential risks using weighted factors:

```python
from safla.core.safety_validation import RiskFactor, RiskAssessment

def calculate_memory_risk(data):
    usage_ratio = data.get('memory_usage', 0) / data.get('memory_limit', 1000000000)
    if usage_ratio < 0.5: return 0.1
    elif usage_ratio < 0.7: return 0.3
    elif usage_ratio < 0.9: return 0.7
    else: return 0.95

memory_risk = RiskFactor(
    name="memory_risk",
    weight=0.3,
    calculator=calculate_memory_risk
)

risk_result = await risk_assessment.assess_risk(operation_data)
if risk_result.overall_score > 0.7:
    recommendations = await risk_assessment.get_mitigation_recommendations(risk_result)
```

guru_safla-complete_571.title: Rollback System
guru_safla-complete_571.content:
Safe reversion through automated checkpoint creation:

```python
from safla.core.safety_validation import CheckpointManager, CheckpointType

checkpoint_manager = CheckpointManager(
    storage_path="./checkpoints",
    max_checkpoints=50,
    compression_enabled=True,
    encryption_enabled=True
)

guru_safla-complete_585.title: Create checkpoint
guru_safla-complete_585.content:
checkpoint_id = await checkpoint_manager.create_checkpoint(
    name="before_major_update",
    checkpoint_type=CheckpointType.MANUAL,
    include_components=["memory_system", "meta_cognitive_engine"]
)

guru_safla-complete_606.title: Emergency Stop System
guru_safla-complete_606.content:
```python
from safla.core.safety_validation import EmergencyStopSystem, StopReason

emergency_stop = EmergencyStopSystem(
    response_time_ms=100,
    graceful_shutdown_timeout=30.0,
    force_shutdown_timeout=5.0
)

guru_safla-complete_617.title: Register triggers
guru_safla-complete_617.content:
emergency_stop.register_trigger(
    name="memory_overflow",
    condition=lambda state: state.get('memory_usage', 0) > 0.95,
    priority=1
)

guru_safla-complete_624.title: Manual trigger
guru_safla-complete_624.content:
await emergency_stop.trigger_emergency_stop(
    reason=StopReason.MANUAL,
    message="Manual emergency stop",
    immediate=True
)
```

guru_safla-complete_634.title: Delta Evaluation System
guru_safla-complete_634.content: Formal framework for quantifying system improvements and measuring progress toward goals.

guru_safla-complete_638.title: Delta Formula
guru_safla-complete_638.content:
```
Delta_total = alpha_1 x Delta_performance + alpha_2 x Delta_efficiency + alpha_3 x Delta_stability + alpha_4 x Delta_capability
```

Where:
- **Delta_performance**: (current_reward - previous_reward) / tokens_used
- **Delta_efficiency**: (current_throughput - previous_throughput) / resource_used
- **Delta_stability**: 1 - divergence_score (with trend analysis)
- **Delta_capability**: new_capabilities / total_capabilities

guru_safla-complete_650.title: Delta Categories
guru_safla-complete_650.content:
| Category | Metrics |
|----------|---------|
| **Performance Deltas** | Speed, Accuracy, Efficiency, Throughput |
| **Quality Deltas** | Reliability, Consistency, Robustness, Maintainability |
| **Learning Deltas** | Knowledge, Skill, Adaptation, Insight |
| **Resource Deltas** | Memory, CPU, Storage, Network |

guru_safla-complete_659.title: Implementation
guru_safla-complete_659.content:
```python
from safla.core.delta_evaluation import DeltaEvaluator

evaluator = DeltaEvaluator()

result = evaluator.evaluate_delta(
    performance_data={
        'current_reward': 0.92,
        'previous_reward': 0.85,
        'tokens_used': 1000
    },
    efficiency_data={
        'current_throughput': 150,
        'previous_throughput': 120,
        'resource_used': 0.8
    },
    stability_data={'divergence_score': 0.15},
    capability_data={'new_capabilities': 2, 'total_capabilities': 10},
    context="performance_critical"
)

print(f"Total Delta: {result.total_delta}")
print(f"Improvement Detected: {result.is_improvement()}")
```

guru_safla-complete_686.title: Threshold Management
guru_safla-complete_686.content:
Adaptive thresholds based on context:

| Type | Base Threshold |
|------|---------------|
| Minimal | 1% improvement |
| Significant | 5% improvement |
| Substantial | 15% improvement |
| Breakthrough | 30% improvement |

Thresholds adjust based on risk level, resource availability, time pressure, and strategic importance.

guru_safla-complete_891.title: Integration Patterns
guru_safla-complete_891.content:
#### 1. Autonomous Agent Pattern

```python
class AutonomousAgent:
    def __init__(self):
        self.memory = HybridMemoryArchitecture()
        self.cognition = MetaCognitiveEngine()
        self.safety = SafetyValidationFramework()

    async def process_task(self, task):
        # Self-awareness check
        awareness = await self.cognition.assess_self_awareness()

        # Create goal
        goal = await self.cognition.create_goal_from_task(task)

        # Strategy selection
        strategy = await self.cognition.select_strategy(task.context)

        # Safety validation
        for action in strategy.actions:
            validation = await self.safety.validate_action(action)
            if validation.is_safe:
                result = await self.execute_action(action)
                await self.memory.store_experience(action, result)

        # Learning and adaptation
        await self.cognition.adapt_from_feedback(task.feedback)
```

#### 2. Collaborative Agent Pattern

```python
class CollaborativeAgent:
    def __init__(self, agent_id):
        self.agent_id = agent_id
        self.mcp_client = FastMCPClient()
        self.shared_memory = HybridMemoryArchitecture()

    async def collaborate_on_task(self, task, other_agents):
        # Share context
        context_embedding = await self.encode_context(task)
        await self.shared_memory.store_shared_context(context_embedding)

        # Coordinate through MCP
        coordination_result = await self.mcp_client.call_tool(
            "agent_coordinator",
            {"task": task, "agents": other_agents}
        )

        # Execute and share results
        my_subtask = coordination_result.assignments[self.agent_id]
        result = await self.process_subtask(my_subtask)
        await self.shared_memory.store_collaborative_result(result)
```

#### 3. Learning Agent Pattern

```python
class LearningAgent:
    def __init__(self):
        self.memory = HybridMemoryArchitecture()
        self.cognition = MetaCognitiveEngine()
        self.delta_evaluator = DeltaEvaluationSystem()

    async def continuous_learning_loop(self):
        while True:
            performance = await self.delta_evaluator.evaluate_current_state()
            opportunities = await self.cognition.identify_learning_gaps()

            for opportunity in opportunities:
                learning_action = await self.design_learning_experiment(opportunity)
                result = await self.execute_learning_action(learning_action)
                await self.memory.consolidate_learning(learning_action, result)
                await self.cognition.update_strategies(result)

            await asyncio.sleep(learning_interval)
```

guru_synaptic-mesh-complete_27.title: Executive Summary
guru_synaptic-mesh-complete_27.content: The **Synaptic Neural Mesh** is a revolutionary distributed neural fabric that fundamentally changes how AI systems coordinate and evolve. It creates a **peer-to-peer network of adaptive neural micro-networks** where intelligence emerges through distributed coordination rather than centralized processing.

guru_synaptic-mesh-complete_31.title: Core Vision
guru_synaptic-mesh-complete_31.content:
Each node in the mesh contains:
- **QuDAG**: Quantum-resistant DAG network for secure P2P messaging
- **ruv-FANN**: Fast neural networks compiled to WebAssembly with SIMD optimization
- **DAA**: Dynamic Agent Architecture for evolutionary behavior
- **Neural Mesh**: Distributed cognition coordination layer

guru_synaptic-mesh-complete_39.title: Key Innovations
guru_synaptic-mesh-complete_39.content:
1. **Ephemeral Intelligence**: Neural networks that exist only as needed
2. **Quantum-Resistant Security**: Post-quantum cryptography (ML-DSA, ML-KEM)
3. **WASM Neural Processing**: Universal deployment with SIMD acceleration
4. **Distributed Consensus**: QR-Avalanche algorithm for Byzantine fault tolerance
5. **Self-Evolving Topology**: Mesh adapts and optimizes autonomously

guru_synaptic-mesh-complete_125.title: 1. QuDAG Core - Quantum-Resistant P2P Networking
guru_synaptic-mesh-complete_125.content:
**Purpose**: Provides secure P2P messaging backbone with DAG-based consensus

**Features**:
- QR-Avalanche consensus algorithm
- ML-DSA and ML-KEM post-quantum cryptography
- Dark domain addressing system
- P2P discovery with Kademlia DHT
- ChaCha20-Poly1305 onion routing for anonymity

**Security Levels**:
- Level 1: ML-KEM-512 (128-bit security)
- Level 3: ML-KEM-768 (192-bit security) - Default
- Level 5: ML-KEM-1024 (256-bit security)

guru_synaptic-mesh-complete_141.title: 2. ruv-FANN - Neural Networks with WASM
guru_synaptic-mesh-complete_141.content:
**Purpose**: Lightweight neural network engine compiled to WebAssembly

**Features**:
- SIMD-accelerated matrix operations
- WebGPU support for GPU acceleration
- 27+ neural architectures (MLP, LSTM, CNN, Transformer)
- Memory-efficient model representation
- <100ms decision latency

**Supported Architectures**:
- MLP (Multi-Layer Perceptron) - Basic feedforward networks
- LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory) - Sequence processing
- CNN (Convolutional Neural Networks) - Image/pattern recognition
- Transformer - Complex reasoning tasks
- Configurable custom architectures

guru_synaptic-mesh-complete_159.title: 3. Neural Mesh - Distributed Cognition Layer
guru_synaptic-mesh-complete_159.content:
**Purpose**: Coordinates distributed neural processing across the mesh

**Features**:
- Inter-network communication protocols
- Distributed learning algorithms
- Neural consensus mechanisms
- Global state synchronization
- Cross-agent learning protocols

guru_synaptic-mesh-complete_170.title: 4. DAA Swarm - Dynamic Agent Architecture
guru_synaptic-mesh-complete_170.content:
**Purpose**: Manages self-organizing agent behavior and evolution

**Features**:
- Self-organizing swarm behavior
- Economic resource allocation
- Fault tolerance and recovery
- Agent lifecycle management
- Byzantine fault tolerance

guru_synaptic-mesh-complete_305.title: Security Features
guru_synaptic-mesh-complete_305.content:
#### Quantum Resistance
- **Level 1**: ML-KEM-512 (128-bit security)
- **Level 3**: ML-KEM-768 (192-bit security) - Default
- **Level 5**: ML-KEM-1024 (256-bit security)

#### Anonymous Routing
- Each hop encrypts the message
- No single node knows the full path
- Traffic timing obfuscation
- Variable path lengths

#### Shadow Addresses
- Temporary identifiers
- Automatic rotation
- Unlinkable to real identity
- Geographic distribution

#### Traffic Obfuscation
- Random padding
- Fake traffic injection
- Timing randomization
- Size normalization
